examples.text: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ زَكَّىٰهَا / وَقَدْ خَابَ مَن دَسَّىٰهَا
examples.english: He has succeeded who shares his boons, / And he has failed who hoards it

senses.gloss: to purify, to cleanse by having performed a ritual

examples.text: خُذْ مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ صَدَقَةً تُطَهِّرُهُمْ وَتُزَكِّيهِم بِهَا وَصَلِّ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّ صَلَوَاتَكَ سَكَنٌ لَّهُمْ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
examples.english: Take charity from their wealth, wherewith thou mayst cleanse and purify them, and pray for them. Lo! thy prayer is an assuagement for them. God listens and notes.

examples.text: فَلَا تُزَكُّوٱ أَنْفُسَكُمْ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱتَّقَى
examples.english: Therefor ascribe not purity unto yourselves. He is Best Aware of him who wardeth off (evil).

etymology.text: Borrowed from Aramaic זַּכֵּי (zakkē). In effect, the same as a denominal verb from زَكَاة (zakāh).
